description: We work with our agencies and partners to support the transport network that helps the UK’s businesses and gets people and goods travelling around the country. We plan and invest in transport infrastructure to keep the UK on the move. DfT is a ministerial department, supported by 23 agencies and public bodies .

details.body:
We work with our agencies and partners to support the transport network that helps the UK’s businesses and gets people and goods travelling around the country. We plan and invest in transport infrastructure to keep the UK on the move.

DfT is a ministerial department, supported by 23 agencies and public bodies.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: UK airlines say that they are not currently seeing a shortage of jet fuel. The government is working with industry and international partners to keep passengers moving.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Jet fuel and travel plans: what you need to know

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The 12 winning projects will provide more people with healthier and sustainable active travel options across England.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Innovative walking, wheeling and cycling projects win share of £1 million

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Modernising track and train will help improve the passenger experience, while supporting jobs, growth and homes in Britain.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Multimillion-pound competition drives cutting-edge innovation in rail industry

ordered_featured_documents.summary: New Cambridge South rail station opening in June 2026.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Next stop, Cambridge South: new station's opening date revealed

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Funding will help develop a numberplate ID system for drones, helping police better protect UK skies and prosecute illegal users.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Nearly £50 million government backing to power up drone and flying taxi tech and crack down on illegal drones

ordered_featured_documents.summary: There are no immediate supply issues, but government is preparing now to give families long-term certainty and avoid unnecessary disruption.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Government sets out plans to protect summer holidays from disruption

ordered_featured_links.title: Who can get a Blue Badge?
Apply for an older person's bus pass

details.body:
Responsible for the overall management of the department. The Permanent Secretary sits on the department’s board.

The role of the department’s board is to:

  • ensure effective governance and properly manage risks
  • shape and direct a shared agenda for the department as a whole, both to deliver ministers’ priorities and to build capability
  • monitor performance and risk, making choices (or recommendations to ministers) on priorities/risk appetite

role.title: Interim Permanent Secretary

role_appointments.title: Jo Shanmugalingam - Interim Permanent Secretary

details.body:
The main areas of responsibility are:

  • aviation policy including airports, airspace, environment, consumers, international connectivity, future of flight, security and safety
  • maritime policy including infrastructure, environment, trade, skills, security and safety
  • aviation, maritime and borders analysis
  • security and resilience of the transport network
  • global leadership on international aviation and maritime, including through the International Civil Aviation Organisation (ICAO), EUROCONTROL and the International Maritime Organization (IMO)
  • sponsorship of arm’s length bodies including the Civil Aviation Authority (CAA), Maritime and Coastguard Agency (MCA) and the Accident Investigation Branches

details.body:
The Director General for Policy, Prisons, Offenders and Analysis is responsible for the department’s policy work alongside the Director General for Courts & Access to Justice. Together they support the Permanent Secretary in their role as principal policy advisor and work closely with the MOJ’s agencies and other justice partners to deliver our outcomes. The policy portfolio covers:

  • Analysis
  • Parliament & Private Office
  • Prison Policy
  • Youth Justice & Offender Policy

details.body:
The Director General for Rail Strategy and Services Group will contribute to the collective leadership of the Department for Transport as Board and Executive Committee members and leaders of the Rail Group.

He has overall responsibility for:

  • developing strategy and policy for the rail sector in the context of broader government policy
  • managing the department’s overall expenditure on rail services and infrastructure
  • ensuring delivery of major projects and programmes including the:
    • £5.7 billion Intercity Express Programme
    • £7 billion Thameslink programme
    • £14.8 billion Crossrail programme (jointly with Transport for London)
  • delivering the ongoing programme of rail franchises
  • effective sponsorship of:
    • British Transport Police
    • Network Rail
    • Office of Rail and Road
    • Rail Accidents Investigation Branch
    • Transport Focus

role.title: Director General Rail Strategy and Services Group

role_appointments.title: Conrad Bailey - Director General Rail Strategy and Services Group

details.body:
The Public Transport and Local Group leads the department’s integrated approach to public transport, enabling economic and productivity growth, improving the user experience, and ensuring value for the taxpayer.

The Public Transport and Local Group coordinates the department’s engagement and collaboration with devolved and local authorities on transport matters, ensuring public transport systems across the country work in the interest of local areas.

role.title: Director General Public Transport and Local Group

role_appointments.title: Conrad Bailey - Director General Public Transport and Local Group

details.body:
To contribute to the collective leadership of  the Department for Transport (DfT ) as a Board and Executive Committee member and lead the Roads Transport Group. The main areas of responsibility are: 

  • ensuring the department’s resources and plans align with our business plan
  • development of policy in relation to roads, motoring, road and vehicle safety, vehicle autonomy, vehicle decarbonisation, traffic, sustainable travel, logistics, supply chains and the border
  • managing the government funding of, and relationship with, Transport for London
  • client management of National Highways and its Roads Investment programme
  • sponsorship of  DfT’s motoring agencies (VCA, DVSA and DVLA)
  • sponsorship of Active Travel England, an agency of the DfT

role.title: Director General Road Transport Group

details.body:
The Chief Scientific Adviser (CSA):

  • provides advice and independent challenge to ministers and senior officials
  • ensures that science and engineering evidence and advice is robust, relevant and high quality
  • ensures there are mechanisms for science and engineering to underpin policymaking and the delivery of services and operations
  • assures the operation of the principles of scientific advice to government to all external scientific advice
  • is responsible for the ‘DfT science, innovation and technology plan’
  • works with CSAs in other departments to share good practice across government and identify and resolve cross-departmental science issues
  • is the Head of the Science and Engineering Profession (HoSEP) at Department for Transport

role.title: DfT Chief Scientific Adviser

details.body:
Non-executive directors provide an external perspective on DFID’s work, while knowing and understanding DFID as an organisation.

They use their experiences and skills from outside DFID to provide objective and constructive challenges on the decisions made by DFID’s committees and boards.

The Non-executive Director sits on the following committees and boards within DFID:

  • the Departmental Board

  • the Executive Management Committee

  • the Audit Committee (chair)

Non-executive directors form a network across government, led by the Government Lead Non-executive.

role.title: Non-executive Director

details.description:
Please use the contact form wherever possible to get in touch with the Department for Transport.

We aim to respond within 20 working days.

We may send your personal data to other transport organisations if they are best placed to answer your enquiry. Find out more about how DfT looks after any personal data you send us at https://www.gov.uk/dft/privacy-policy.

London

details.title: Office address and general enquiries

details.description:
The Department for Transport press office only deals with enquiries from the media.

For personal enquiries regarding MOT, please email: enquiries@dvsa.gov.uk.

If you are not a member of the media, use the switchboard line:
 
0300 330 3000
 
If you are a member of the media and have a media query contact the press office: https://www.gov.uk/government/organisations/department-for-transport/about/media-enquiries.

The Secretary of State has overall responsibility for all Department for Transport business, including: 

  • overarching responsibility for the departmental portfolio and oversight of the ministerial team 
  • delivering the transport priorities of the government: making transport cheaper, greener, and more reliable 
  • ensuring the transport network is safe and accessible 
  • corporate functions such as oversight of departmental finance and public appointments

details.body:
The Secretary of State has overall responsibility for all Department for Transport business, including: 

  • overarching responsibility for the departmental portfolio and oversight of the ministerial team 
  • delivering the transport priorities of the government: making transport cheaper, greener, and more reliable 
  • ensuring the transport network is safe and accessible 
  • corporate functions such as oversight of departmental finance and public appointments

This is not a current government role

The minister is responsible for:

  • rail strategy (including overall fares policy)
  • London - including Crossrail
  • HS2
  • international and European engagement
  • aviation – including Air Accident Branch

The Minister is responsible for:

  • rail (including commercial, operational performance and community rail)
  • buses (including concessionary fares)
  • taxis
  • light rail and trams
  • smart ticketing (including rail fares and ticketing)
  • traffic management
  • regional and local transport (including local roads, maintenance, major projects, winter resilience)
  • natural environment and climate change
  • cleaner fuel and vehicles
  • sustainable travel (including walking and cycling)
  • accessibility and equalities
  • alternatives to travel
  • SMEs and growth

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Transport

details.body:
The Minister is responsible for:

  • strategic roads and the Highways Agency
  • motoring agencies
  • road safety and standards
  • freight and logistics – including lorry road user charging
  • maritime and dangerous goods (including the Maritime and Coastguard Agency)
  • London
  • Crossrail
  • better regulation

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Transport

details.body:
Responsible for the overall management of the department. The Permanent Secretary sits on the department’s board

The role of the department’s board is to:

  • ensure effective governance and properly manage risks
  • shape and direct a shared agenda for the department as a whole, both to deliver ministers’ priorities and to build capability
  • monitor performance and risk, making choices (or recommendations to ministers) on priorities/risk appetite

To contribute to the collective leadership of DfT as a Board and Executive Committee member and lead the corporate group. The main areas of responsibility are:

  • corporate functions - finance, HR, property, corporate procurement, IT, communications, shared services
  • change management
  • corporate governance
  • internal audit
  • performance management of DfT’s motoring agencies
The Legal Director’s responsibility is to provide legal advice to the Department for Transport and its agencies.

To contribute to the collective leadership of DfT as a Board and Executive Committee member and lead the Domestic Group. The main areas of responsibility are:

  • ensuring the Department’s resources and plans align with our business plan
  • preparatory work on High Speed Rail
  • the Government’s response to the recommendations proposed by Sir Roy McNulty and subsequent proposals for the reform of the rail industry
  • development of policy in relation to roads, traffic, road safety, bus travel and logistics
  • the Department’s relationship with local government and its contribution to the localism and Big Society agendas

The main areas of responsibility are:

  • ensuring that the development of a post-pandemic international travel system supports the UK global ambitions
  • aviation policy including airports, environment, consumer protection, security and safety
  • maritime policy including infrastructure, environment, security and trade
  • space launch
  • security and safety of the transport network
  • global leadership in international aviation, as the UK Director General for Civil Aviation, including for the International Civil Aviation Organisation (ICAO), G7 and Eurocontrol
  • international negotiations with other governments and international transport organisations
  • sponsorship of arm’s length bodies including the CAA, Maritime and Coastguard Agency and the Accident Investigation Branches

ordered_roles.title: Director General Aviation, Maritime, International and Security

details.body:
To contribute to the collective leadership of DfT as a Board and Executive Committee member and lead the Roads, Traffic and Local Group. The main areas of responsibility are:

  • ensuring the department’s resources and plans align with our business plan
  • development of policy in relation to roads, traffic, road safety, sustainable travel, bus travel and logistics
  • managing the department’s relationship with local government and its contribution to the localism agenda
  • managing the government funding of, and relationship with, Transport for London
  • sponsorship of DfT’s motoring agencies

ordered_roles.title: Director General Roads Traffic and Local Group

details.body:
Responsibilities are:

As Director General, xxx has overall responsibility for all aspects of the developing High Speed 2.

To contribute to the collective leadership of the Department for Transport as a Board and Executive Committee member and lead of the Rail Group.

To contribute to the collective leadership of the Department for Transport as a Board and Executive Committee member and lead of the Rail Group.

The Director General, Rail Group has overall responsibility for:

  • developing strategy and policy for the rail sector in the context of broader government policy
  • managing the department’s overall expenditure on rail services and infrastructure
  • ensuring delivery of major projects and programmes including the:
    • £5 billion Intercity Express Programme
    • £6 billion Thameslink programme
    • £14.7 billion Crossrail programme (jointly with Transport for London)
  • delivering the ongoing programme of rail franchises
  • effective sponsorship of:
    • Network Rail
    • Office of Rail and Road
    • Rail Accidents Investigation Branch
    • British Transport Police

The main areas of responsibility are:

  • leadership at Director General level for the management of financial and human resources
  • the development of organisational capability and strategy for the transport system and the government’s role within it
  • Principal Finance Officer of DfT, ensuring effective financial control and planning of budget around £13 billion
  • Head of Profession for finance staff across the whole DfT Group

The Minister is responsible for:

  • accessibility across all modes of transport
  • HS2 policy and legislation
  • rail
  • rail security
  • smart ticketing

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Transport

details.body:

  • HS2 – Phase Two

  • localism and devolution

  • accessibility and equalities

  • SMEs

The Minister is responsible for:

  • high speed rail (HS2)
  • aviation
  • Europe and international
  • maritime
  • devolution
  • cycling and walking

  • provides advice and independent challenge to ministers and senior officials
  • ensures that science and engineering evidence and advice is robust, relevant and high quality
  • ensures there are mechanisms for science and engineering to underpin policymaking and the delivery of services and operations
  • assures the operation of the principles of scientific advice to government to all external scientific advice
  • is responsible for the ‘DfT science, innovation and technology plan’

  • development of policy in relation to roads, traffic, road safety, sustainable travel, bus travel and logistics

details.body:
To contribute to the collective leadership of the Department for Transport as a Board and Executive Committee member and lead of the Rail Executive. The main areas of responsibility are:

  • the government’s response to the recommendations proposed by Sir Roy McNulty and subsequent proposals for the reform of the rail industry
  • delivery of major rail investment projects, involving cross industry leadership
  • ensuring rail passenger franchises deliver customer benefits and that financial and other risks are effectively managed
  • sponsorship of Directly Operated Rail and the British Transport Police

To contribute to the collective leadership of  the Department for Transport (DfT ) as a Board and Executive Committee member and lead the Roads Transport Group. The main areas of responsibility are:

Non-executive board members provide the Department for Transport with strategic advice, support and challenge. They also attend departmental board meetings and meet with the management team.

The Secretary of State for Transport appointed Sir Jeremy Sullivan to oversee the consultation process on the draft Airports National Policy Statement and ensure best practice is upheld.

Sir Jeremy’s role was to:

  • consider the government’s plans for raising awareness of the consultation and for stakeholder and public engagement during this period, challenging these where necessary
  • monitor the delivery of the consultation and make recommendations for improvements to the Secretary of State for Transport
  • produce a report to the Secretary of State for Transport on the government’s approach to and delivery of the consultation

Contact

If you have any comments on the consultation process, please contact Sir Jeremy at independentadviser@runwayconsultation.gsi.gov.uk.

Sir Jeremy will not comment or respond on any issue relating to government policy on airport expansion, but will consider all comments he receives on the consultation process.

ordered_roles.title: Independent consultation adviser to the draft Airports National Policy Statement

The Acting Director General, Rail Group has overall responsibility for:

  • developing strategy and policy for the rail sector in the context of broader government policy

David leads and oversees the DfT portfolio of major rail investment projects (excluding High Speed 2). This portfolio includes Northern Powerhouse Rail and the Integrated Rail Plan, the Transpennine Route Upgrade, Restoring Your Railway, other Network Rail enhancements, East West Rail and Crossrail.

The Rail Infrastructure Group works closely with the Rail Strategy and Services Group and High Speed Rail Group to provide a fully integrated approach to delivering rail investment.
Non-executive board members provide the Department for Transport with strategic advice, support and challenge. They also attend departmental board meetings and meet with the management team.

The Second Permanent Secretary General:

  • works with the Permanent Secretary on all aspects of the department’s leadership
  • has specific responsibility for cross-cutting issues such as decarbonisation, security and economic growth
  • is responsible for the department’s work with the aviation and maritime sectors

The main areas of responsibility are:

  • aviation policy including airports, airspace, environment, consumers, international connectivity, future of flight, security and safety
  • maritime policy including infrastructure, environment, trade, skills, security and safety
  • aviation, maritime and borders analysis
  • security and resilience of the transport network
  • global leadership on international aviation and maritime, including through the International Civil Aviation Organisation (ICAO), EUROCONTROL and the International Maritime Organization (IMO)
  • sponsorship of arm’s length bodies including the Civil Aviation Authority (CAA), Maritime and Coastguard Agency (MCA) and the Accident Investigation Branches

details.body:
To contribute to the collective leadership of  the Department for Transport (DfT ) as a Board and Executive Committee member and lead the Rail Services Group.

The Director General Rail Services Group is responsible for the delivery of all rail passenger services and a portfolio of rail infrastructure projects.

They are part of the sector leadership, including the Network Rail CEO, and industry managing directors and owning groups, working to shape the future of the rail sector in the UK ahead of the creation of Great British Railways (GBR).

To contribute to the collective leadership of  the Department for Transport (DfT) as a Board and Executive Committee member and lead the Roads Transport Group. The main areas of responsibility are: 

  • ensuring the department’s resources and plans align with our business plan

  • managing the government funding of, and relationship with, Transport for London

details.body:
The Public Transport and Local Group leads the department’s integrated approach to public transport, enabling economic and productivity growth, improving the user experience, and ensuring value for the taxpayer.

The Public Transport and Local Group coordinates the department’s engagement and collaboration with devolved and local authorities on transport matters, ensuring public transport systems across the country work in the interest of local areas.

details.body:
Responsible for the overall management of the department. The Permanent Secretary sits on the department’s board.

The role of the department’s board is to:

  • ensure effective governance and properly manage risks
  • shape and direct a shared agenda for the department as a whole, both to deliver ministers’ priorities and to build capability
  • monitor performance and risk, making choices (or recommendations to ministers) on priorities/risk appetite

The Minister for Aviation, Maritime and Decarbonisation is responsible for:   

  • aviation and airport expansion 
  • maritime

  • freight and borders 
  • international
  • security
